name: Mediterranean Diet
note: Olive oil, vegetables, fish, legumes, moderate wine; gold standard for cardiovascular and cognitive health

name: Anti-Inflammatory Diet
note: Emphasizes omega-3s, polyphenols, and fiber while minimizing processed foods, refined carbs, and industrial seed oils

name: Low-FODMAP Diet
note: Reduces fermentable carbohydrates for IBS and SIBO symptom management; 6-8 week elimination then reintroduction

name: AIP Diet
note: Strict elimination removing grains, legumes, dairy, eggs, nightshades, nuts, and seeds; autoimmune support

name: Paleo Diet
note: Meat, fish, eggs, vegetables, fruit, nuts; eliminates grains, legumes, dairy, and processed foods

name: Ketogenic Diet
note: Very low carb (<50g/day), high fat; induces ketosis; therapeutic for epilepsy; used for metabolic health

name: Low-Histamine Diet
note: Restricts fermented, aged, and leftover foods; used for histamine intolerance and MCAS

name: Low-Oxalate Diet
note: Restricts spinach, nuts, and other high-oxalate foods; kidney stone prevention and vulvodynia interest

name: Gluten-Free Diet
note: Eliminates wheat, barley, rye; required for celiac disease; chosen by many with gluten sensitivity

name: Dairy-Free Diet
note: Eliminates all dairy products; used for lactose intolerance, casein sensitivity, and inflammatory conditions

name: Carnivore Diet
note: Animal products only; used by some for autoimmunity, gut healing, and weight loss; strict elimination approach

name: Plant-Based Diet
note: Emphasizes whole plant foods; reduces chronic disease risk; ensure B12, iron, zinc, omega-3 supplementation

name: Elimination Diet
note: Removes top allergens (gluten, dairy, eggs, soy, corn, nuts) for 3-6 weeks then systematic reintroduction

name: GAPS Diet
note: Gut healing protocol emphasizing bone broth, fermented foods, and animal fats; staged introduction over 18-24 months

name: SCD
note: Eliminates complex carbohydrates; used for IBD and SIBO; monosaccharides and specific foods allowed

name: Low-Salicylate Diet
note: Reduces salicylates found in many plants; used for aspirin sensitivity, asthma, and behavior in children

name: Low-Sulfur Diet
note: Reduces high-sulfur foods (eggs, garlic, cruciferous veg); used for CBS gene variants and H2S SIBO

name: Intermittent Fasting 16:8
note: 16-hour fast with 8-hour eating window; activates autophagy; common start time is 12-8pm eating window

name: Time-Restricted Eating 14:10
note: Gentler form of time-restricted eating; 10-hour eating window aligned with circadian rhythm; good starting point

name: OMAD
note: One meal per day; aggressive TRE; ensure adequate nutrition in single eating window; not for all populations

name: 5:2 Fasting Protocol
note: Eat normally 5 days, restrict to 500-600 calories 2 non-consecutive days; flexible fasting approach

name: Alternate Day Fasting
note: Alternates full-day fast with normal eating; significant caloric restriction; challenging to maintain

name: Eating Window Tracking
note: Logging first and last bite times; aligning food intake with daylight hours optimizes circadian metabolism

name: Daily Water Intake Goal
note: General guideline: half body weight in ounces; adjust for activity, climate, and sweat rate

name: Electrolyte Hydration Protocol
note: Adding sodium, potassium, and magnesium to water; especially important on low-carb, high activity, or heat

name: Mineral Water
note: Natural mineral water provides trace minerals; Gerolsteiner and San Pellegrino are mineral-rich sources

name: Structured Water
note: Theoretically ordered water structure; advocated by Gerald Pollack; evidence limited but popular in wellness

name: Filtered Water
note: Removing chlorine, fluoride, heavy metals, and microplastics from tap water; Berkey, Clearly Filtered popular

name: Increase Fiber Intake
note: Target 25-35g daily from vegetables, legumes, fruits, and whole grains; gut microbiome and metabolic health

name: Increase Fermented Foods
note: Kimchi, sauerkraut, kefir, yogurt, kombucha daily; increases microbiome diversity (Sonnenburg lab research)

name: Increase Omega-3 Rich Foods
note: Prioritize fatty fish 3x/week, walnuts, flaxseeds, chia seeds; reduce omega-6:omega-3 ratio

name: Increase Leafy Greens
note: Aim for 2+ cups daily; folate, magnesium, vitamin K, and antioxidants; varied greens for diversity

name: Increase Cruciferous Vegetables
note: Broccoli, cauliflower, Brussels sprouts, kale; NRF2 activation, estrogen detox, and cancer chemoprotection

name: Reduce Processed Sugar
note: Limit added sugar to <25g/day (WHO guideline); reduce inflammatory load and blood glucose variability

name: Reduce Refined Carbohydrates
note: Replace refined grains with whole food alternatives; reduces glycemic variability and insulin response

name: Reduce Inflammatory Oils
note: Eliminate canola, soybean, corn, and sunflower oils; replace with olive oil, coconut oil, and butter

name: Reduce Alcohol
note: Even moderate alcohol increases breast cancer risk; impairs sleep quality; liver burden; hormonal effects

name: Reduce Caffeine
note: Cut after noon for sleep quality; gradual taper to avoid withdrawal headaches; adrenal recovery protocol

name: Reduce Histamine Foods
note: Avoid aged cheese, wine, fermented products, leftovers, avocado; used for histamine intolerance and MCAS

name: Reduce Oxalate Foods
note: Limit spinach, almonds, beets, dark chocolate; kidney stone prevention and tissue oxalate accumulation

name: Reduce FODMAP Foods
note: Gradual reduction of high-FODMAP foods with systematic reintroduction to identify personal triggers

name: Bone Broth
note: Rich in collagen peptides, glycine, glutamine, and minerals; gut healing and joint support; simmer 12-24 hours

name: Collagen Broth
note: Extended simmer broth high in gelatin (gels when cooled); skin, gut, and joint nutrient density

name: Beef Liver
note: Most nutrient-dense food per calorie; B12, folate, iron, copper, CoQ10, vitamin A; aim 4oz 1-2x/week

name: Organ Meats
note: Liver, heart, kidney, and tongue; nutrient-dense beyond muscle meat; ancestral and functional nutrition approach

name: Sardines
note: Excellent omega-3, calcium (with bones), vitamin D, CoQ10, and protein; low mercury; sustainable

name: Wild-Caught Salmon
note: High EPA/DHA, astaxanthin, vitamin D, and protein; choose wild over farmed for better nutrient profile

name: Blueberries
note: Highest antioxidant berry; anthocyanins for brain, cardiovascular, and gut microbiome support; wild berries preferred

name: Berries Protocol
note: Blueberries, raspberries, strawberries, blackberries daily; polyphenols feed gut bacteria and reduce inflammation

name: Dark Chocolate
note: 70%+ cacao; flavanols for cardiovascular and cognitive health; 1 oz daily optimal; choose low-cadmium brands

name: Green Tea
note: EGCG catechins; metabolism, cancer chemoprotection, and cognitive support; 2-3 cups daily

name: Matcha
note: Whole leaf green tea powder; higher EGCG and L-theanine than steeped green tea; calm focus

name: Herbal Teas
note: Chamomile, peppermint, ginger, nettle, rooibos; therapeutic phytochemicals without caffeine

name: Medicinal Mushrooms Diet
note: Shiitake, maitake, oyster, lion's mane; beta-glucans, ergothioneine, and immune support; sauté or roast

name: Kimchi
note: Fermented Napa cabbage; Lactobacillus-rich; gut microbiome diversity and immune support; spicy probiotic food

name: Sauerkraut
note: Raw unpasteurized fermented cabbage; rich in L. plantarum and vitamin K2; gut health and digestion

name: Kefir
note: Fermented milk beverage with 10-50 strains; clinical evidence for gut health; also dairy-free water kefir

name: Raw Garlic
note: Crush and let sit 10 minutes before eating to maximize allicin; antimicrobial, cardiovascular, and prebiotic

name: Ginger Tea
note: Steep fresh ginger slices in hot water; anti-nausea, anti-inflammatory, and digestive support

name: Turmeric Latte
note: Turmeric with warming spices in milk; add black pepper and fat for curcumin bioavailability

name: Seed Cycling
note: Specific seeds in follicular and luteal phases to support estrogen and progesterone; flax/pumpkin days 1-14; sesame/sunflower days 15-28

name: AG1
note: 75-ingredient greens powder with vitamins, adaptogens, and probiotics; mix in water; expensive but popular all-in-one

name: LMNT Electrolytes
note: 1000mg sodium, 200mg potassium, 60mg magnesium per packet; no sugar; popular on low-carb and fasting protocols

name: Liquid IV
note: Cellular transport technology; 2x hydration vs water alone; convenient travel and illness hydration

name: Nuun Tablets
note: Effervescent electrolyte tablets; lower calories than traditional sports drinks; travel-friendly

name: Vital Proteins Collagen
note: Grass-fed bovine collagen peptides; 18g per serving; stirs into coffee or smoothies; skin and joint support

name: Great Lakes Gelatin
note: Hydrolyzed (dissolves cold) or gelatin (gels hot) forms; same bovine collagen amino acids

name: Kettle and Fire Bone Broth
note: Shelf-stable packaged bone broth; convenient alternative to homemade; collagen-rich chicken and beef varieties

name: Primal Kitchen Products
note: Avocado-oil based condiments and sauces; paleo and keto friendly; no industrial seed oils

name: Perfect Keto Products
note: Keto-focused supplements including MCT oil powder, collagen bars, and exogenous ketones

name: Bulletproof Coffee
note: Coffee blended with grass-fed butter and MCT oil; fat-based breakfast replacement; cognitive focus claimed

name: MCT Oil
note: Rapidly converted to ketones; C8 (caprylic) is most ketogenic; start with 1 tsp to avoid GI distress

name: Chia Seeds
note: Omega-3 ALA, fiber, and hydration; 1-2 tbsp daily; gel in liquid; supports bowel regularity

name: Flaxseeds Ground
note: Grind fresh for best nutrition; lignans (phytoestrogen), ALA omega-3, and soluble fiber; 1-2 tbsp daily

name: Hemp Seeds
note: Complete protein with all essential amino acids; GLA omega-6; easily digested; sprinkle on food

name: Walnuts
note: Highest ALA omega-3 nut; polyphenols for gut microbiome; handful daily associated with longevity

name: Avocado
note: Monounsaturated fat, potassium, folate, and lutein; enhances absorption of fat-soluble carotenoids from salads

name: Olive Oil Extra Virgin
note: Oleocanthal anti-inflammatory; polyphenols for cardiovascular health; use cold or low-heat; store in dark bottle

name: Fermented Soy Products
note: Miso, tempeh, natto — fermented forms of soy with probiotic and nutritional benefits unlike unfermented soy

name: Raw Honey
note: Antimicrobial enzymes preserved in raw form; local raw honey for allergy support; Manuka for UMF rating

name: Apple Cider Vinegar
note: Dilute 1-2 tbsp in water before meals; blood glucose support; never undiluted (enamel erosion)

name: Coconut Oil
note: Contains lauric acid and MCTs; stable at high heat; antimicrobial properties; use in cooking or topically

name: Ghee
note: Clarified butter; casein and lactose removed; butyrate content; high smoke point for cooking

name: Nutritional Yeast
note: Rich in B vitamins (fortified); complete protein; beta-glucans; popular vegan flavor and nutrition booster

name: Spirulina Powder
note: Protein, B12-like (not truly bioavailable B12), iron, and antioxidants; add to smoothies; verify heavy metal testing

name: Chlorella Powder
note: Broken cell wall form for absorption; chlorophyll, minerals, and detox support; start low for GI tolerance

name: Wheatgrass Juice
note: Fresh-pressed shot; highly alkalizing; chlorophyll-rich; best consumed immediately after juicing

name: Moringa Leaf Powder
note: Iron, calcium, protein, and antioxidants; mild flavor; add to smoothies or stir into food

name: Sea Vegetables
note: Nori, wakame, dulse, kelp; natural iodine, trace minerals, and fucoidans; use in moderation for thyroid support

name: Prebiotic Foods Protocol
note: Jerusalem artichokes, garlic, onions, leeks, asparagus, green banana; feeds beneficial gut bacteria

name: Rainbow Diet Protocol
note: 30+ different plant foods per week (Sonnenburg lab target); each color = different phytonutrients

name: Protein Optimization Protocol
note: Target 1g+ per pound lean body mass; distribute across meals (30-40g per meal to hit leucine threshold)

name: Organ Meat Capsule Supplement
note: Desiccated liver, heart, and kidney capsules for those who dislike organ meat taste; nutrient-dense food

name: Kombucha
note: Fermented tea beverage; acetic acid, B vitamins, and some beneficial microbes; low sugar brands preferred

name: Coconut Yogurt
note: Dairy-free probiotic yogurt; check for live active cultures; lower protein than dairy but gut-friendly

name: Grass-Fed Beef
note: Higher CLA, omega-3, and fat-soluble vitamins than grain-fed; supports regenerative agriculture

name: Pasture-Raised Eggs
note: Higher vitamin D, omega-3, and carotenoids than conventional eggs; whole egg includes choline in yolk

name: Raw Milk
note: Controversial; retains enzymes and beneficial bacteria; legal status varies; safety risk with immunocompromised

name: Tempeh
note: Fermented soybeans; complete protein, B12 precursors, and beneficial bacteria; firmer and more nutrient-dense than tofu

name: Miso
note: Fermented soybean paste; probiotic-rich when unpasteurized; do not boil to preserve cultures

name: Natto
note: Richest dietary source of vitamin K2 (MK-7); nattokinase enzyme; acquired taste; prebiotic and probiotic

name: Pumpkin Seeds
note: Rich in zinc, magnesium, and plant omega-6; antiparasitic; used in seed cycling luteal phase

name: Sunflower Seeds
note: Vitamin E and selenium; used in seed cycling luteal phase (days 15-28)

name: Sesame Seeds
note: Sesamin lignans for estrogen metabolism; calcium; used in seed cycling luteal phase

name: Cold-Pressed Juicing
note: Concentrated micronutrients without fiber; best from cold-press; drink immediately; low fruit to reduce sugar

name: Anti-Candida Diet
note: Eliminates sugar, yeast, fermented foods, and refined carbs; used for candida overgrowth protocols

name: Phase 1 Liver Support Foods
note: B vitamins, antioxidants (vitamin C, E), cruciferous; supports CYP450 phase 1 liver enzymes

name: Phase 2 Liver Support Foods
note: Sulfur foods (garlic, onion, cruciferous), glycine, glutamine; supports phase 2 conjugation pathways

name: Hormone-Supportive Diet
note: High fiber, cruciferous vegetables, fermented foods, healthy fats; reduces excess estrogen circulation

name: Blood Sugar Balancing Protocol
note: Protein + fat + fiber with each meal; avoid carbs alone; walking after meals; CGM feedback useful

name: Mitochondrial Support Diet
note: CoQ10-rich foods, B vitamins, magnesium, and alpha-lipoic acid; emphasis on nutrient-dense whole foods

name: Sleep-Supportive Eating
note: Avoid large meals 2-3 hours before bed; tart cherry, kiwi, and turkey (tryptophan) support melatonin production

name: Brain-Supportive Nutrition
note: MIND diet (Mediterranean + DASH hybrid); emphasizes berries, leafy greens, nuts, olive oil, and fish

name: Beam Dream Protein
note: Sleep-supportive protein powder blend; magnesium and reishi; taken before bed

name: Onnit Total Human
note: Comprehensive supplement day and night packs from Onnit; includes adaptogens, omega-3, and multivitamin

name: Organifi Green Juice
note: Organic green powder blend; ashwagandha, chlorella, moringa, spirulina; mixes in water

name: Nutraceutical Food Protocol
note: Systematic use of specific foods at therapeutic quantities for health outcomes; integrative nutrition approach
